evals.prompt: I'm about to build a user authentication system with login, registration, and password reset. Can you create a class diagram for it before I start coding? The key entities are: Users, Sessions, and email-based password reset tokens.
evals.expected_output: A class diagram YAML at diagram/auth-system.yaml with User, Session, and ResetToken classes, including attributes like email, passwordHash, token, expiry, and methods like register, login, validatePassword, isExpired. Relationships: Session belongs to User, ResetToken belongs to User.

assertions.description: Diagram contains User, Session, and ResetToken classes
User has email and passwordHash; ResetToken has token and expiry
All relationship from/to refs match existing class names

evals.prompt: Look at the dashboard code in dashboard/ and create a class diagram for it. I want to understand how config.py and main.py relate to each other.
evals.expected_output: A class diagram at diagram/dashboard.yaml reverse-engineered from the actual dashboard code, showing classes/functions from config.py and main.py with their relationships.

assertions.description: Classes reflect actual code in config.py and main.py
Config loading/saving from config.py is represented
FastAPI app/endpoints from main.py are represented
All relationship from/to refs match existing class names

evals.prompt: Design the class structure for a simple shopping cart system. We need Products, a Cart that holds CartItems, and an Order that gets created when you check out. Each Product has a name, price, and stock quantity. The Cart should be able to add/remove items and calculate a total.
evals.expected_output: A class diagram at diagram/shopping-cart.yaml with Product, Cart, CartItem, Order classes. Cart composition→CartItem, CartItem association→Product, Order association→Cart.

assertions.description: Diagram contains Product, Cart, CartItem, Order classes
Product has name, price, stockQuantity attributes
All relationship from/to refs match existing class names
